description: Headway University degree (Pakistan-origin diploma mill)

expectedOutput.reasoning: Headway University is a documented unaccredited online operation in the Belford / Rochville mill family. Degrees granted with no academic work; not recognized by any USDOE-recognized accreditor or any country's Ministry of Education registry.

source: Oregon ODA unaccredited list; FTC consumer alert

description: Hill University degree (Wyoming mill closed by AG)

expectedOutput.reasoning: Hill University was an unaccredited Wyoming operator shut down after Wyoming raised licensure standards in 2009. Same legislative cleanup that ended Kennedy-Western. Pre-2010 PhDs from Wyoming-domiciled "universities" without USDOE accreditation are mill artifacts.

description: University of Northern Virginia degree (closed for I-20 fraud)

expectedOutput.reasoning: University of Northern Virginia (UNVA) lost SCHEV authorization and ICE SEVP certification in 2011 after a federal raid for visa fraud. Pre-closure degrees existed but the institution was operating as a visa mill rather than a credentialing institution. Post-2011 UNVA credentials are facially fraudulent.

description: Hartland University degree (life-experience model)

extractedFields.programNotes: PhD awarded based on resume + work experience; no dissertation defense

expectedOutput.reasoning: A PhD awarded without dissertation defense or supervised research is structurally impossible at any USDOE-recognized institution. Hartland's self-described "no dissertation" model is the textbook diploma-mill life-experience scheme.

source: FTC educational fraud guidance; Oregon ODA

description: NPI with 11 digits (one too many)

expectedOutput.reasoning: NPI is exactly 10 digits per CMS specification (45 CFR 162.406). An 11-digit value cannot be a valid NPI; this is structurally impossible and indicates fabrication or transcription error severe enough to require source verification.

description: Massachusetts medical license with non-standard format

extractedFields.issuerName: Massachusetts Board of Registration in Medicine

expectedOutput.reasoning: MA Board of Registration in Medicine issues physician licenses as 6-digit numbers (e.g., 123456). "BRM-99" mimics agency initialism rather than the issued license format and would not match the BoRiM verification portal.

description: DEA registration with invalid first letter (J prefix)

expectedOutput.reasoning: DEA registration number first letter encodes registrant type (A/B/F/M/P/R for practitioners/distributors/MLPs/etc.). "J" is not a valid registrant-type prefix per the DEA specification, so the registration is structurally invalid before checksum.

source: DEA Office of Diversion Control format spec

description: CPA license issued by a non-existent state body

expectedOutput.reasoning: NY CPA licenses are issued by the New York State Education Department - Office of the Professions, not by a "New York State CPA Authority" (no such agency). Either a fabricated issuer name or a transcription error against the actual NYSED Office of the Professions registry.

description: Conferral date in February (off-cycle for the issuer)

expectedOutput.reasoning: Princeton University awards undergraduate degrees only at the late-May/June commencement (with rare off-cycle conferrals for specific cases). A February conferral date is anomalous and warrants registrar verification — could indicate template tampering where the issue date was edited.

description: Transcript with negative credit hour entry

expectedOutput.reasoning: Negative credit hours are mathematically impossible on a US transcript. A single -3 line entry indicates spreadsheet manipulation where a course was retroactively erased by subtracting credits from the total. Indiana University would never produce this output.

description: Department name with a single-letter typo (gives away template)

expectedOutput.reasoning: UT Austin's engineering school is "Cockrell School of Engineering" (one l). The triple-l "Cockrelll" is the kind of typo that survives Photoshop-level template tampering when the forger doesn't notice the OCR-fed extra glyph. Real UT Austin diplomas never spell it that way.

description: NPI returns provider in unrelated state

extractedFields.crossRefNote: NPPES returns this NPI registered to a provider with primary practice address in Vermont

expectedOutput.reasoning: NPI cross-reference returns a Vermont primary practice address while the credential claims Texas licensure. Could indicate (a) a real provider with a stolen TX license number, (b) a real TX provider whose NPI was misappropriated, or (c) a synthetic credential. All three need investigation; in none does the credential pass identity verification.

description: Bar registration registered to a different person (last-name mismatch)

expectedOutput.reasoning: NY OCA attorney directory verification returns a different family name for the same registration number. First name + middle initial match is suggestive of a same-person name change, but absent a recorded name-change order with OCA, the credential cannot be presented as currently valid under "Brennan."

description: Credential claims active status but issuer revocation list shows surrender

priorActions: Voluntary Surrender 2022-04-30 in lieu of further discipline

expectedOutput.reasoning: A voluntary-surrender-in-lieu action is a discipline equivalent — the licensee gave up the license to avoid further proceedings. Presenting the license as "Active" after a 2022 surrender is materially false. MBC verification would return surrendered/inactive.

description: Real Cornell PhD in a department that exists, but field misrepresented as a sister-program

expectedOutput.reasoning: Cornell's ORIE department is real and the credential itself is genuine. The misrepresentation occurs when the holder later claims the PhD as "Computer Science" — a different Cornell department with different admissions and qualifying-exam standards. Real institution + real degree + tampered field-of-study representation is a sophisticated-fraud signature for hiring fraud.

description: CFA charter date claimed before charter awarded

expectedOutput.reasoning: CFA charter is awarded only after Level III passage and the four-year work-experience requirement. Charter date 2019-08-15 with Level III pass 2020-08-12 is impossible — you cannot be granted the charter before passing the qualifying exam. Either the charter date or the Level III date was tampered.

description: Real ACGME-accredited program but holder's residency dates fall outside program approval window

expectedOutput.reasoning: Stanford Internal Medicine Residency is a real ACGME-accredited program. However, claiming residency dates of 2011-07-01 → 2014-06-30 against a program that didn't receive ACGME approval until 2013-01-01 means the first 18 months would not count toward board eligibility. Either the program-approval window is wrong (verifiable) or the residency dates were inflated to claim seniority.

description: Genuine Stanford CS Master degree (no fraud)

expectedOutput.reasoning: Stanford University is a regionally accredited research institution (founded 1885, current). Computer Science is an established Stanford program in the School of Engineering. June 2024 conferral aligns with the standard Stanford academic calendar. No fraud indicators.

description: Genuine Florida medical license with correct ME prefix (no fraud)

expectedOutput.reasoning: Florida DOH issues physician licenses with prefix "ME" + 6 digits. License "ME134567" matches that pattern. Issuance year 2020 falls in active issuance period. Status active. No format violations.

description: Genuine BLS provider card (no fraud)

expectedOutput.reasoning: AHA-issued BLS Provider cards are valid for 2 years from issuance. The expiry exactly 2 years out matches AHA policy. No format issues.
